title: U.S. South Pacific Scholarship Program (USSP)
description: U.S. State Department-funded scholarship for Bachelor's and Master's degree study at the University of Hawai'i, for students from Pacific Island nations including Fiji, Papua New Guinea, Samoa, and Tonga. Covers tuition, a monthly allowance, and other expenses. Recurs annually — check the East-West Center's site for the current cycle's exact deadline.
org: U.S. Department of State / East-West Center
visaNote: J-1 exchange visitor visa sponsored by the program.

title: Princeton in Asia (PiA) Fellowship
description: Paid, immersive work placements at host organizations across Asia — potential locations include Vietnam, Mongolia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Cambodia, Indonesia, and others, assigned through the placement process rather than chosen by the applicant. A living stipend covers housing, food, transportation, and communications; visa expenses are reimbursed. Recurs annually with applications typically opening in late August and closing around November 1. Listed here under Vietnam as a representative destination — actual placement varies.
visaNote: Visa expenses incurred in the U.S. before departure are reimbursed; specific visa type depends on host country.
